LAW AND MOTION, 302, CROSS DEFENDANT PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT AS A SLAPP SUIT IS DENIED. PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY ("PIIC") HAS MET ITS INITIAL THRESHOLD BURDEN TO SHOW THAT THE 3RD THROUGH 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION AROSE FROM THE DEFENDANT'S FREE SPEECH OR PETITION ACTIVITY, AND MR. HUWYLER HAS MET HIS SHIFTING BURDEN OF SHOWING THE PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. (CCP 425.16(A); HAIGHT ASHBURY FREE CLINICS, INC. V. HAPPENING HOUSE VENTURES (2010) 184 CAL.APP.4TH 1539, 1547.) THE POLICY DOES NOT UNEQUIVOCALLY EXCLUDE PLAINTIFF AS AN INSURED. (BIRKHIMER DECL., EXHIBIT H.) CIIP'S OWN POLICY SPECIFICALLY PROVIDED A WAIVER OF THE RIGHT TO RECOVERY FROM HUWYLER. (BIRKHIMER DECL., EXHIBIT E, C10, P. 3.) DESPITE THIS WAIVER, PIIC SUED HUWYLER FOR SUBROGATION. TO THE EXTENT THAT CIIP CITES TO SPECIFIC PORTIONS OF THE POLICY BY PAGE NUMBER, CIIP HAS NOT PRESENTED A BATES STAMPED COPY OF THE POLICY, AND THEREFORE THE ARGUMENT IS NOT SUPPORTED BY THE EVIDENCE CITED. (SEE, E.G., POINTS & AUTH., P. 10:5; NAJEMY DECL., EXHIBIT H.) THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312). JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/EHG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-28-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSHUA NAJEMY OF THE ROBINSON DI LANDO, APLC, TEL. NO. (213) 229-0100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF/CROSS-COMPLAINANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ALLEN J. KENT, TEL. NO. 272-2662 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT/ CROSS-DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT AS A SLAPP SUIT is Denied. Philadelphia Indemnity Insurance Company ("PIIC") has met its initial threshold burden to show that the 3rd through 6th causes of action arose from the defendant's free speech or petition activity, and Mr. Huwyler has met his shifting burden of showing the probability of prevailing on the merits. (CCP 425.16(a); Haight Ashbury Free Clinics, Inc. v. Happening House Ventures (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539, 1547.) The Policy does not unequivocally exclude Plaintiff as an insured. (Birkhimer Decl., Exhibit H.) CIIP's own policy specifically provided a waiver of the right to recovery from Huwyler. (Birkhimer Decl., Exhibit E, C10, p. 3.) Despite this Waiver, PIIC sued Huwyler for subrogation. To the extent that CIIP cites to specific portions of the Policy by page number, CIIP has not presented a bates stamped copy of the Policy, and therefore the argument is not supported by the evidence cited. (See, e.g., Points & Auth., p. 10:5; Najemy Decl., Exhibit H.) The objections are overruled. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312). Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/EHG)
